title: English – 4th Grade (Year 2)
description: Second year of English – 4th grade Portugal – Aprendizagens Essenciais
descriptionPt: 2.º ano de Inglês – 4.º ano de escolaridade – Aprendizagens Essenciais

modules.title: All About Me (Expanded)
topics: Review greetings + new: Good evening, Nice to meet you, See you later, See you tomorrow
Personal information: name, age, birthday, nationality, where you live – I live in Lisbon. I'm Portuguese.
Describing yourself: I'm tall/short. I have got brown eyes and short hair. I'm funny/kind/shy.
Describing friends: She is my best friend. He is tall. She has got long hair. He is clever and kind.
Likes and preferences: My favourite colour/food/animal/sport is… I like… but I don't like…
objectives: Give extended personal information
Describe yourself and friends physically and personality
Use adjectives for personality: kind, funny, clever, shy, brave, friendly
Express likes, dislikes and preferences
Have a short conversation about yourself

modules.title: Numbers, Dates & Time
topics: Numbers 20 to 100: twenty, thirty, forty… one hundred. twenty-one, thirty-five, etc.
Ordinal numbers: 1st to 31st – first, second, third… thirty-first
Months of the year: January to December. My birthday is in March. When is your birthday?
The date: What's the date today? It's the 9th of March. Today is Monday, the 9th of March.
Telling the time: What time is it? It's 3 o'clock / half past 7 / quarter past 9 / quarter to 5.
objectives: Count and use numbers up to 100
Use ordinal numbers correctly
Name all 12 months and ask about birthdays
Ask and tell the full date
Tell the time with o'clock, half past, quarter past/to

modules.title: School Subjects & Timetable
topics: School subjects: English, Maths, Portuguese, Music, Art, PE, Science, History, ICT
School spaces: classroom, playground, library, canteen, gym, toilet, sports field, hall
Timetable: On Monday I have English and Maths. On Friday I have PE and Music.
Expressing preferences: What's your favourite subject? My favourite subject is Art. I like PE because it's fun.
School routine: School starts at 9 o'clock. I have lunch at 12. School finishes at 3:30.
objectives: Name school subjects and spaces
Describe a weekly timetable
Express subject preferences with reasons
Tell the time in a school routine context
Use present simple for habits: I have, I go, I finish

modules.title: My House & Furniture
topics: Rooms: bedroom, bathroom, kitchen, living room, dining room, garden, garage, hall
Furniture: bed, table, chair, sofa, TV, fridge, cooker, wardrobe, lamp, mirror, bath, shower, desk, bookshelf
There is / There are: There is a big sofa in the living room. There are three bedrooms. There isn't a garage.
Prepositions of place: in, on, under, behind, next to, between, in front of, near
Describing your house: I live in a house/flat. My bedroom is small. My favourite room is the kitchen.
objectives: Name all rooms and common furniture
Use there is / there are (affirmative, negative, question)
Describe where objects are using prepositions
Describe your home in 5–6 sentences
Ask and answer Is there…? / Are there…?

modules.title: Daily Routines
topics: Morning routine: I wake up at 7. I get up. I have a shower. I get dressed. I have breakfast. I brush my teeth. I go to school.
After school: I have lunch. I do my homework. I play with my friends. I watch TV. I read a book.
Evening routine: I have dinner. I take a bath. I put on my pyjamas. I go to bed. I sleep.
Present simple: I wake up at 7. She wakes up at 8. He goes to school at 8:30. We have lunch at 12.
Adverbs of frequency: always, usually, sometimes, never – I always have breakfast. I never watch TV in the morning.
objectives: Describe a full daily routine in sequence
Use present simple with I/he/she/we/they
Tell the time for each routine activity
Use adverbs of frequency (always, usually, sometimes, never)
Compare your routine with a friend's routine

modules.title: My Town & Directions
topics: Places in town: school, park, hospital, supermarket, cinema, bookshop, library, museum, church, pharmacy, restaurant, bakery, post office, police station, bus stop, train station
Where is…? Where is the hospital? It's next to the park. It's near the school. It's far from here.
Giving directions: Go straight. Turn left. Turn right. Cross the street. It's on the left/right.
Shops and shopping: I buy bread at the bakery. I buy books at the bookshop. I buy medicine at the pharmacy.
Transport: car, bus, train, bicycle, on foot, plane, boat – I go to school by bus. I go to the park on foot.
objectives: Name at least 12 places in a town
Ask and answer Where is the…?
Give and follow simple directions
Associate shops with what they sell
Talk about transport using by… / on foot

modules.title: Countries, Nationalities & Languages
topics: Countries: Portugal, England/UK, USA, Brazil, France, Spain, Italy, Germany, China, Japan, Australia, Canada, India
Nationalities: Portuguese, English/British, American, Brazilian, French, Spanish, Italian, German, Chinese, Japanese, Australian, Canadian, Indian
Languages: I speak Portuguese and English. He speaks French and Spanish. They speak Chinese.
Where are you from? I'm from Portugal. I'm Portuguese. She's from France. She's French.
Culture: flags, capital cities (London, Washington, Paris…), traditional food, famous places (Big Ben, Eiffel Tower, Statue of Liberty…)
objectives: Name countries, nationalities and languages
Ask and answer about origin using Where are you from?
Match countries to capitals, flags and landmarks
Use he/she is from… He/She is… (nationality)
Show awareness of cultural diversity

modules.title: Food & Healthy Eating
topics: Food groups: fruit, vegetables, meat, fish, dairy, cereals, sweets
Expanded vocabulary: pasta, rice, soup, salad, yogurt, cereal, toast, ham, tuna, beans, peas, carrot, tomato, lettuce, onion, cucumber
Drinks: water, milk, juice, tea, hot chocolate – Water is the best drink. Juice has sugar.
Meals and habits: For breakfast I usually have cereal and milk. For lunch I have soup and fish. For dinner I have rice and chicken.
Healthy vs unhealthy: You should eat fruit and vegetables. You shouldn't eat too many sweets. Fruit is healthy. Crisps are unhealthy.
objectives: Name foods from each food group
Describe meals using present simple
Use should / shouldn't for healthy eating advice
Distinguish healthy and unhealthy food with reasons
Express food preferences within a conversation

modules.title: Clothes & Weather
topics: Clothes: T-shirt, shirt, trousers, jeans, shorts, dress, skirt, jumper, coat, jacket, hat, scarf, gloves, boots, shoes, trainers, socks, pyjamas, uniform, swimsuit
Weather: sunny, cloudy, rainy, windy, snowy, hot, cold, warm, foggy, stormy – What's the weather like? It's sunny and hot.
Seasons and clothing: In winter I wear a coat and boots. In summer I wear shorts and a T-shirt. In autumn I wear a jacket.
Present continuous – wearing: I'm wearing a blue T-shirt. She's wearing a red dress. What are you wearing?
Shopping for clothes: Can I have a T-shirt, please? What size? Small/Medium/Large. How much is it? It's 10 euros.
objectives: Name at least 15 items of clothing
Ask and answer about the weather
Connect seasons, weather and clothing choices
Use present continuous to describe what people are wearing
Role-play a simple shopping dialogue

modules.title: Animals, Habitats & Nature
topics: Wild animals expanded: lion, tiger, elephant, giraffe, monkey, bear, wolf, snake, dolphin, whale, eagle, penguin, parrot, shark, crocodile, zebra, hippo
Habitats: jungle/rainforest, ocean/sea, desert, Arctic/Antarctic, farm, forest, river
Describing animals: A dolphin lives in the sea. It can swim fast. It's grey. It eats fish. It hasn't got legs.
Comparisons: An elephant is bigger than a dog. A cheetah is faster than a horse. A whale is the biggest animal.
Endangered animals: Some animals are in danger. We should protect nature. Don't throw rubbish in the sea.
objectives: Name wild animals and their habitats
Describe animals using has got, can, lives in, eats
Make simple comparisons: bigger, faster, smaller
Use the superlative: the biggest, the fastest
Show awareness of environmental responsibility

modules.title: Sports, Hobbies & Free Time
topics: Sports: football, basketball, tennis, swimming, cycling, running, gymnastics, surfing, skating, karate, volleyball
Hobbies: reading, drawing, painting, playing video games, playing music, cooking, dancing, singing, collecting stamps/stickers
After school / weekends: On Saturdays I play football. In the afternoon I ride my bike. At the weekend I visit my grandma.
Expressing frequency: I always play football. I usually read before bed. I sometimes go swimming. I never play tennis.
Likes and abilities: I love swimming! I'm good at football. She's not very good at tennis. He likes playing the piano.
objectives: Name common sports and hobbies
Talk about what you do in your free time
Use adverbs of frequency with activities
Express likes/abilities: I love…, I'm good at…
Describe weekend activities using present simple

modules.title: Festivities & Traditions
topics: Christmas traditions: Christmas dinner, crackers, stockings, carols, presents under the tree – In England they eat turkey at Christmas.
Easter traditions: Easter egg hunt, hot cross buns, spring – In the UK children do an Easter egg hunt.
Halloween: trick or treat, costumes, jack-o'-lantern, haunted house – Halloween is popular in the USA and UK.
Comparing cultures: In Portugal we celebrate Carnival. In the UK they have Pancake Day/Shrove Tuesday. In the USA they celebrate Thanksgiving.
Invitations and cards: Would you like to come to my party? Dear… Happy Birthday / Merry Christmas / Happy Easter. Love, …
objectives: Describe traditions for major English-speaking celebrations
Compare Portuguese and English/American traditions
Use specific vocabulary for each celebration
Write a simple greeting card or invitation
Talk about what people do during celebrations

modules.title: Grammar in Action
topics: Verb to be – all persons: I am, you are, he/she/it is, we are, they are + negatives + questions
Have got / Has got – all persons + negatives + questions: She has got a cat. They haven't got a garden. Has he got a sister?
Present simple – daily routines & habits: He plays football. She doesn't like fish. Do they go to school? Does she speak English?
Present continuous – now: I'm reading. She's writing. They aren't playing. What are you doing? Is he sleeping?
Question words: What, Where, Who, When, How, How many, How much, How old, Why, What time – and forming full questions
objectives: Use to be in affirmative, negative and interrogative
Use have got / has got with all persons
Distinguish present simple (habits) from present continuous (now)
Form negative sentences and questions correctly
Use a variety of question words to ask for information

modules.title: Reading & Writing Projects
topics: Reading: short stories, dialogues, postcards, e-mails, menus, posters, simple instructions
Writing about yourself: a short paragraph (6–8 sentences) – My name is… I'm 9. I live in… I have got… I like… My favourite…
Writing about others: My friend's name is… She is… She has got… She likes… She can…
Describing a picture: I can see… There is/are… The boy is playing… The girl is wearing…
Connectors: and, but, because, then – I like pizza but I don't like fish. I go to bed early because I'm tired. First I have breakfast, then I go to school.
objectives: Read and understand short illustrated texts
Write a 6–8 sentence paragraph about yourself
Write a short description of a friend or family member
Describe a picture using there is/are and present continuous
Use connectors and, but, because, then to link ideas
